app.name: MohsenTracker
app.tagline: Become your next version. Build the habits. See the future.

onboarding.welcomeBody: Tiny daily actions compound into the person you become. Let's design who you're becoming, then build the habits that get you there.
onboarding.languageBody: You can change this anytime in settings.
onboarding.futureSelfNameTitle: What's the name of your future self?
onboarding.futureSelfNameBody: A label for who you're becoming. Something like "{example}". You'll see this every day.
onboarding.visionTitle: Who do you want to become in 1 year?
onboarding.visionBody: Describe the person you want to be in 2–3 sentences. We'll show this on your dashboard so you remember why you started.
onboarding.visionPlaceholder: I am someone who is consistent, healthy, and present for the people I love…
onboarding.whyTitle: Why does this matter to you?
onboarding.whyBody: When motivation fades, your reason is what keeps you going. Write it like you'd say it out loud.
onboarding.whyPlaceholder: Because the next decade should look different from the last one — I want to become someone I'm proud of…
onboarding.signInBody: Use Google or your email to sign in. Your habit ticks will count toward the live community totals — and you can switch devices without losing data later.
onboarding.signInDisabled: Authentication isn't configured yet (Firebase isn't set up). You can continue without signing in.
onboarding.categoriesBody: Start with what feels right. You can change this later.
onboarding.habitsBody: Tap to keep or drop each habit. You can always add more from /habits/new.
onboarding.habitsNoCategories: Pick at least one category first.

home.noHabits: You haven't added any habits yet.
home.allDone: All habits done for today. Beautiful work.
home.livesCaption: Below 50% loses a heart · 3 days above 50% earns one back

islamicVerse.arabic: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُحِبُّ ٱلتَّوَّٰبِينَ وَيُحِبُّ ٱلْمُتَطَهِّرِينَ
islamicVerse.translation: Indeed, Allah loves those who turn to Him in repentance, and He loves those who purify themselves.

install.title: Install MohsenTracker
install.androidBody: Install the app for fullscreen use, faster loads, and offline habit logging.

splash.words: What you tell yourself becomes the truth you live by.
splash.actions: Each action is a vote for who you're becoming.
splash.habits: A habit is a seed you plant — bad or good. Better it grow good.
splash.character: You are what you repeat. Not what you intend.
splash.destiny: Today is the only day you actually have. Use it.

auth.guestNote: Quick try, no email. Your data stays on this device — sign in later to keep it across devices.
auth.guestHint: No email — sign out and sign back in with Google or email to save your data.
auth.toggleToSignUp: Don't have an account? Create one
auth.notSignedInTitle: Sign in to enable live counts
auth.notSignedInBody: Once signed in, your habit ticks count toward the live community totals shown next to each habit.

errors.userNotFound: No account found for that email.
errors.emailInUse: An account with this email already exists.
errors.weakPassword: Password must be at least 6 characters.
errors.invalidEmail: That doesn't look like a valid email.
errors.disabled: Authentication isn't configured. Set up Firebase first.

habit.name: Habit name
habit.replacementHint: What good habit will you do instead?
habit.presetsBody: One-tap add for the habits you don't have yet.

progress.title: Progress
progress.noStreaks: Complete a habit today to start a streak.

dailyBars.title: Last 14 days
dailyBars.body: Daily completion rate. Today is highlighted on the right.

strengths.title: Strong vs weak
strengths.body: Average completion across the last 30 days. Strongest categories first.

ramadan.title: Ramadan

preBanner.title: Ramadan in {n} days
preBanner.body: Two weeks to prepare. Practice Mondays and Thursdays fasting, ease into Quran reading, settle the things you'd want behind you when the month begins.

lastTenBanner.title: Last ten nights
lastTenBanner.body: Tahajjud, Quran, dua, sadaqah. Better than a thousand months is somewhere in here.

shawwalBanner.title: Six fasts of Shawwal
shawwalBanner.body: Whoever fasts Ramadan and follows it with six fasts in Shawwal — as if they fasted the whole year.

iftar.title: Iftar at {time}
iftar.note: Time is your local Maghrib estimate — adjust below until the prayer-times API ships.

trackers.fastsHint: Tap as you complete each day.
trackers.juzHint: Aim 1 per day = full Quran in the month.

lastTenSection.title: Laylat al-Qadr nights
lastTenSection.body: Mark each odd night you stayed up worshipping. Better than a thousand months.
lastTenSection.duaArabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ عَفُوٌّ تُحِبُّ الْعَفْوَ فَاعْفُ عَنِّي
lastTenSection.duaTranslation: O Allah, You are Pardoning, You love to pardon, so pardon me.
lastTenSection.duaSource: Reported in Sunan al-Tirmidhi 3513 and Sunan Ibn Majah 3850 — taught by the Prophet ﷺ to Aisha (R)

items.increaseQuran: Read a few extra pages of Quran daily
items.settleDebts: Settle debts and ask forgiveness from those you've wronged
items.stockKitchen: Stock the kitchen with simple, healthy iftar foods
items.setRamadanGoals: Set 2–3 goals you want to leave Ramadan with

homeShifted.title: Ramadan dashboard
homeShifted.body: Other categories are still in Categories, just out of the way for the month.

ramadan.noActiveBody: Ramadan mode auto-activates on the first of Ramadan. You can force it on from Profile → Theme to preview.

reset.title: Dopamine Reset
reset.intro: Step back from a stimulus that's running you. Let your baseline come back.

weekly24h.label: 24-hour weekly
weekly24h.body: A short fast you can repeat each week. Ideal for screens and sweets.

monthly7d.label: 7-day monthly
monthly7d.body: A week without the thing. Long enough to feel the difference.

quarterly30d.label: 30-day quarterly
quarterly30d.body: A real reset. Enough time to break the loop and rebuild the self.

deep90d.label: 90-day deep
deep90d.body: For serious addictions. Three months of compounding clarity.

stageHint.cravings: Your brain is asking for the easy hit. Hardest stretch — stay close to your replacements.
stageHint.adjustment: The pull weakens. Sleep gets better, focus comes back in flashes.
stageHint.clarity: The fog lifts. You start seeing what the stimulus was hiding.
stageHint.restoration: Baseline returning. Small things feel rewarding again.
stageHint.integration: The new normal stabilises. You no longer need willpower the same way.
stageHint.mastery: Identity-level shift. You are someone who doesn't do this anymore.

reset.checkInBody: One a day. Keeps the reset honest.
reset.mood: How do you feel? (1 = struggling, 5 = thriving)
reset.urgesPlaceholder: What did the craving sound like? When did it hit?

activity.journal: Write a page, anything that's on your mind
activity.puzzle: A puzzle, chess problem, or sudoku
activity.learnSomething: Watch a short lecture on something hard
activity.listenLecture: An audiobook or talk you've been meaning to play
activity.readQuran: Read a page of Quran with translation
activity.gratitudeList: List five things you're grateful for
activity.reflectiveSilence: Sit in silence for ten minutes
activity.duaForOthers: Make dua for someone, by name
activity.helpNeighbour: Do a small favour for a neighbour
activity.familyMeal: Eat a meal with your family, no phones

reset.completeBanner: Mashallah — reset complete. The new baseline is yours.
reset.confirmAbandon: End this reset early? Your lifetime clean days stay with you.

relapse.title: It's okay. Try again.
relapse.body: Streaks reset. Your lifetime clean days do not. Be honest, then begin again.
relapse.reflectionPlaceholder: What was the trigger? What can you do differently next time?
relapse.quote: Ten years from now, you'll look back on this moment and remember you chose to stop. The time, energy, and clarity you save by walking away from this — that's the gift you're giving your future self.

reset.homeStartBody: Step back from a stimulus that's running you. The first 24 hours are the hardest.

angerProtocol.title: Anger settling in
angerProtocol.body: Walk these steps in order. Don't rush. The wave passes.
angerProtocol.homeButtonLabel: Anger settling in? Open the protocol.

taawwudh.title: Seek refuge from Satan
taawwudh.body: The Prophet ﷺ said: "If a man seeks refuge with Allah from Satan when angry, his anger will go away." Say it now, even silently.
taawwudh.translation: I seek refuge in Allah from Satan, the accursed.

posture.title: Change your posture
posture.body: If you are standing, sit down. If you are sitting, lie down. Move your body out of the position anger has it in.

wudu.title: Make wudu
wudu.body: "Anger is from Satan, and Satan was created from fire — and fire is extinguished by water. So when one of you is angry, let him make ablution." Cool the heat literally.

breath.title: Be silent. Breathe for 90 seconds.
breath.body: "When one of you is angry, let him be silent." The adrenaline wave in the body lasts about 90 seconds — outlast it. Breathe in slowly through the nose, out slowly through the mouth.

dua.title: Close with a dua
dua.body: Hand the moment back to Allah.
dua.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْغَضَبِ وَالْكَسَلِ
dua.translation: O Allah, I seek refuge in You from anger and laziness.

books.title: Books
books.intro: What you read shapes who you become.
books.noActive: Add a book to start your shelf.
books.noCompleted: Books you finish will land here.

field.author: Author
field.whyReadingPlaceholder: It feels relevant. The author is rigorous. A friend recommended it…

books.tooManyActive: More than 5 books at once is rarely sustainable. Consider finishing one before starting another.
books.paceUnknown: Log a few days to see your pace.
books.completeBody: Capture what you want to remember. You can come back and edit anytime.
books.reviewPlaceholder: What changed in you because of this book?
books.confirmDelete: Delete this book? This can't be undone.

yearReview.title: Year in books
yearReview.compoundLine: {n} this year × 10 = {decade} books in a decade. A different person, one chapter at a time.
yearReview.noBooks: No completed books in this year — yet.

feature.categoryCardBody: Track what you're reading, finish more of it, see your year in books.

habitLink.unlinkedBody: Connect Books to a daily reading habit so book pages count toward your streak.

logSheet.title: Log today's reading
logSheet.body: Pick the book you read and add today's pages.
logSheet.noActive: No book in progress yet — add one to start logging.
logSheet.audiobookNote: Audiobook minutes don't count toward a pages-based streak.

restart.homeBannerTitle: {strikes} days under 50% in the last fortnight
restart.homeBannerBody: The list got too long. Pare it back so the streak can breathe — less is more.
restart.heroTitle: It's not failure. The list got too big.
restart.heroBody: Three days under 50% means the design is wrong, not you. The compound effect only works on habits you actually finish — so let some go for now.
restart.principleBody: The Prophet ﷺ said the most beloved deeds to Allah are the most consistent, even if small. Keep the few you'll do today. You can always add more later when these are automatic.
restart.chooseBody: Tap a habit to drop it. The ones still ticked will be your shorter list. Dropped habits and their logs are removed — by design, this is a fresh start.
restart.noHabits: No daily habits to pare. Add some, give them a couple of weeks, then come back if you over-committed.
restart.summaryWarning: Dropping a habit also removes its history. The streak is gone with it.
restart.confirm: Drop {n} habits and reset the slate?

fasting.title: Fasting
fasting.intro: Fasting isn't just food. The Prophet ﷺ taught that the eyes, ears, hands, and tongue each have a share in the fast. Track all five today.
fasting.categoryCardBody: Log your fast for the eyes, ears, hands, tongue, and food across the day.
fasting.last7Legend: The darker the square, the more parts kept that day.
fasting.hadithArabic: مَنْ لَمْ يَدَعْ قَوْلَ الزُّورِ وَالْعَمَلَ بِهِ فَلَيْسَ لِلَّهِ حَاجَةٌ فِي أَنْ يَدَعَ طَعَامَهُ وَشَرَابَهُ
fasting.hadithTranslation: Whoever does not give up false speech and acting on it — Allah has no need that he give up his food and drink.

eyes.title: Eyes
eyes.body: Lowering the gaze from haram — screens, people, content.

ears.title: Ears
ears.body: No gheebat, no gossip, no music that pulls you under.

hands.title: Hands
hands.body: No harming, no taking what isn't yours, no idle scrolling.

tongue.title: Tongue
tongue.body: No lying, no backbiting, no arguing for the sake of it.

food.title: Food & drink
food.body: The classic fast — from dawn to sunset, or whatever window you keep.

autophagy.title: Autophagy fast
autophagy.intro: Start a timer-based fast (16:8, 18:6, OMAD, or longer). Water only — your body switches into repair mode after the first chunk of hours.
autophagy.categoryCardBody: Start the timer and let your body do its own cleanup work.
autophagy.startBody: Pick a target window. You can end the fast whenever you want — the goal is just to know where you are.
autophagy.targetReached: Target reached — keep going or end it whenever

savings.title: Savings
savings.intro: Log deposits and withdrawals. Small, consistent saving compounds — the year-end number is built one entry at a time.
savings.categoryCardBody: Track every deposit and withdrawal and watch the running total grow.
savings.yearlyDrain: At this pace: {value} drained a year
savings.empty: No entries yet. Add your first deposit above.

debts.title: Debts
debts.intro: Track who owes you and what you owe. A clean ledger keeps relationships clean.
debts.categoryCardBody: Track money you've lent or borrowed and settle it cleanly.
debts.empty: No active debts. Add one above when something new comes up.

settings.title: Profile & Settings
settings.name: Name
settings.ramadanAutoBody: Auto activates on the 1st of Ramadan. Force on to preview the dashboard, off to never switch.
settings.soundHint: Soft chimes when you check off habits, hit streak milestones, claim rewards, finish a book.
settings.angerProtocolHint: Show a one-tap button on Home that opens the Prophetic ﷺ anger protocol: ta'awwudh → posture → wudu → 90 seconds of silence → dua.
settings.prayerNote: Times will populate once the prayer-times API is wired up. Your selection is saved.
settings.notifNote: Push notifications go live when Firebase Cloud Messaging is connected. Until then your preferences are stored locally.
settings.consequenceBody: How blunt should the "what's at stake" panel be on each habit?
settings.readingHabitBody: Pages logged on books count toward this habit's daily total. Streak and heatmap update automatically.
settings.readingHabitNone: None — keep books separate from habits

consequenceHints.full: All consequences with emphasis. Use if you respond to hard truths.

settings.exportHint: Download every habit, log, streak, reward and punishment in one file.
settings.signOutHint: Wipes the local data on this device. Once Firebase is connected this also signs you out everywhere.
settings.signOutConfirm: This wipes all your habits, streaks, rewards and progress on this device. Continue?
settings.resetProgressHint: Wipes every habit, log, streak, reward and book on this device and re-runs onboarding. Your sign-in stays — only the local data resets.
settings.resetProgressConfirm: This wipes every habit, streak, reward and progress on this device and starts you over from onboarding. There's no undo. Continue?
settings.deleteAccountHint: Permanently removes your account and any data we hold about you on the server. The local data on this device is wiped too. This can't be undone.
settings.deleteAccountConfirm: This will permanently delete your account, your sign-in, and any cloud-stored progress. There is no recovery. Continue?
settings.deleteAccountReauth: For security, please sign out and sign in again, then retry the delete.
settings.deleteAccountFailed: Couldn't delete the account. Try again, or contact support.

rewards.title: Rewards & Punishments
rewards.intro: Tiny actions deserve small wins, and a real cost holds them in place.
rewards.rewardsHint: Pick or type things you love. We pull from this list when you earn one.
rewards.punishmentsHint: When you skip a critical habit, one of these activates. Charity is the safe default if your list is empty.
rewards.criticalHint: Mark the habits where missing a day actually matters. These trigger a punishment.
rewards.noCriticalHabits: Mark a habit as critical from its detail page.
rewards.pendingEmpty: Earn one by finishing all of today's habits or hitting a 7 / 30 / 100-day streak.
rewards.activePunishmentsEmpty: Nothing owed. Stay consistent on your critical habits.

tierHint.small: Tiny dopamine the moment you finish all of today's habits.

rewards.noOptionAssigned: Your call — pick anything from this tier.

presets.diningOut: Dinner at a place you love
presets.newHobbyKit: A starter kit for a new hobby

punishmentPresets.charityDonation: Donate a small amount to charity
punishmentPresets.spiritualFastDay: A day of fasting — eyes, ears, hands, tongue, and food
punishmentPresets.autophagyFast: An autophagy fast — at least 16 hours, water only
punishmentPresets.earlyWakeup: Wake up an hour earlier tomorrow
punishmentPresets.noScreensEvening: No screens for the rest of the evening
punishmentPresets.writeApology: Write a sincere apology to someone you've wronged
punishmentPresets.extraQuran: Read an extra page of Quran with translation
punishmentPresets.silenceHour: An hour of silence — no talking, no media
punishmentPresets.handwritePages: Handwrite a page about why you missed today
punishmentPresets.noEntertainment: No movies, games or shows for 24 hours
punishmentPresets.chorePile: Tackle a chore you've been avoiding

rewards.defaultCharity: Donate a small amount to charity (the safe default).

safety.meal: Punishments must never involve skipping meals or starvation.
safety.extreme: Extreme exercise as punishment can cause real harm.
safety.sleep: Sleep deprivation is off the table — health goals come first.
safety.harm: Anything that causes self-harm is not allowed.

rewards.homeBannerBody: Loss-aversion holds habits in place. Add a few wins and a stickK-style cost.

habitDetail.atStake: What's at stake if you stop
habitDetail.reversalLabel: How to reverse it, starting today

replacement.body: When the urge hits, do this instead.

muted.activeReset: Consequence messaging is paused while you're in a dopamine reset.
muted.recentRelapse: Paused after a relapse — be gentle with yourself today.
muted.lowMood: Paused after a low check-in. Come back when you're steadier.

books.title: Your books
books.subtitle: Pages you log here count toward this habit's streak.
books.connectTitle: Connect this habit to your books
books.connectBody: Pages you log on any book will count toward this habit's daily total.

generic.lostYear: Skip it and you forfeit {value} {unit} every year.
generic.lostDecade: A decade off and {decade} {unit} are gone.
generic.yearlyNoUnit: Doing this every day adds up to {days} successful days a year.
generic.lostYearNoUnit: Skip it and you lose {days} successful days a year.
generic.lostDecadeNoUnit: A decade off and you forfeit {decade} successful days.
generic.r1: Start with the smallest possible version of this habit today — even one rep counts.
generic.r2: The body and brain rebuild fast once you give them the right inputs again.

reading.p2: Across a decade, {decadeBooks} books. A small library.
reading.c1: Skip a year and that's {books} books — and the upgraded thinking inside them — that you never get.
reading.c2: A decade of not reading compounds into the same person you are now, only older. Your kids see that, too.
reading.r1: Start with one page tonight before bed — that's it. Tomorrow, two.
reading.r2: Keep a book in every room you sit in. Boredom becomes the trigger.
reading.hadithText: Read! In the name of your Lord who created.

saving.p3: About {thirtyYear} after 30 years — small money becomes big money.
saving.c1: Skip a year and the gap to retirement widens. Money you didn't save is interest that's working against you instead of for you.
saving.c2: A decade off compound growth is roughly the difference between owning your home outright and renting forever.
saving.r1: Automate it. Move {yearly} a year out of your account before you can spend it — pay yourself first.
saving.r2: Cancel one subscription tonight. Funnel that exact amount into savings.
saving.hadithText: And whoever fears Allah, He will make for him a way out, and provide for him from where he does not expect.

steps.p1: Walking {km} km this year — roughly {laps} times around your neighbourhood.
steps.p2: Over a decade, {decadeKm} km on foot — farther than the long way around the planet.
steps.c1: Skip the walks and you forfeit roughly {km} km of free, easy movement every year.
steps.c2: Sedentary years compound into joint stiffness, lower mood, and a measurably higher risk of depression and back pain.
steps.r1: One daily walk this week — fifteen minutes, no headphones if you can.
steps.r2: Stack it on top of something you already do: a walk after dinner, or to the masjid / shop / friend's place instead of driving.
steps.hadithText: Walking to the mosque is one of the deeds whose reward is multiplied with every step.

exercise.p2: About {km} km on foot — like walking London to Paris {trips, plural, =1 {once} other {# times}}.
exercise.p3: Across a decade, {decadeHours} hours of strength built.
exercise.c1: Sedentary years compound into the metabolic risk profile that fills cardiology wards — diabetes, heart disease, the back pain that never quite goes away.
exercise.c2: Muscle and aerobic capacity you don't build in your 30s are very hard to recover in your 60s.
exercise.r1: Walk 10 minutes today. That's the dose. Tomorrow, the same.
exercise.r2: Tie movement to something you already do — push-ups before every shower, calf raises while the kettle boils.
exercise.hadithText: The strong believer is more beloved to Allah than the weak believer, while there is good in both.

quranPages.p2: You complete the Quran every {monthsPerKhatm} months — {khatmsPerYear, plural, =1 {one khatm} other {# khatmas}} a year.
quranPages.p3: Across a decade, {decadeKhatms} khatmas of revelation.
quranPages.c1: A year of not opening the Mushaf is a year your heart drifts further from the Speech of Allah — quietly, almost imperceptibly.
quranPages.c2: A decade away is a different relationship with the book entirely. The door doesn't close, but the ease of return fades.
quranPages.r1: One page after Fajr. That's the whole strategy.
quranPages.r2: Place the Mushaf on top of your prayer mat tonight. Let it greet you in the morning.
quranPages.hadithText: The best of you are those who learn the Quran and teach it.

screenTime.p1: Stay at {limit} hours a day. Cut one hour and reclaim {daysReclaimed} days a year.
screenTime.p2: Across a decade, {decadeDaysReclaimed} days back in your life.
screenTime.c1: Late-night scrolling shifts melatonin, fragments REM, and bleeds into next morning's focus and patience with the people you love.
screenTime.c2: A decade of comparison feeds — every meal somebody else's, every body somebody else's — and the mind starts believing your life is the small one.
screenTime.r1: Charge the phone outside the bedroom tonight. Buy a cheap alarm clock if you need one.
screenTime.r2: Greyscale the screen, delete the worst app off the home row. Make the slot machine boring.
screenTime.hadithText: From the goodness of a man's Islam is leaving what does not concern him.

tahajjud.p1: {nights} nights of closeness with Allah this year.
tahajjud.p2: {decadeNights} nights of tahajjud across a decade.
tahajjud.c1: Each missed night is a private door of mercy that opened — and closed — without you walking through.
tahajjud.c2: Years of sleeping through the last third of the night quietly remove a kind of intimacy that no daytime worship replaces.
tahajjud.r1: Go to bed earlier tonight. Set the alarm for one rakat — that's enough to begin.
tahajjud.r2: If you wake for water or the bathroom, pray two rakats before lying back down.
tahajjud.hadithText: Our Lord descends to the lowest heaven in the last third of the night and says: Who is calling Me, that I may answer him? Who is asking Me, that I may give him?

sadaqah.p2: {decadeCount} acts across a decade — sadaqah jariyah that keeps giving.
sadaqah.c1: Tightness with money compounds into tightness in the chest. The Sunnah on sadaqah is also a Sunnah on the heart.
sadaqah.c2: Years of holding back when there's room to give — small barakah, small horizon.
sadaqah.r1: Round up your next purchase. Donate the difference, however small.
sadaqah.r2: Set a recurring monthly amount, even if it's tiny. Make giving the default.

learning.p2: About {decadeHours} hours over a decade — enough to master a new field.
learning.c1: A year without learning is a year of repeating yesterday. The market shifts; you stay where you are.
learning.c2: A decade of stagnation is the version of you that gets passed over for the version that kept showing up.
learning.r1: Pick one topic that scares you. Spend 15 minutes on it tonight.
learning.r2: Teach what you learn — a friend, a sibling, a journal entry. Teaching is how it sticks.
learning.hadithText: Whoever travels a path in search of knowledge, Allah makes easy for him a path to Paradise.

gheebat.p1: Hold the tongue. {days} days of clean speech this year.
gheebat.p2: Across a decade, that's {decadeDays} days you protected others' honour.
gheebat.c1: Backbiting is a leak in the same bucket your prayers and fasts are filling. The harder you work, the more painful the leak.
gheebat.c2: A decade of careless speech changes how people relax around you — and how you feel walking into a room.
gheebat.r1: When the urge rises, redirect: say something true and good about the person instead.
gheebat.r2: If you've already wronged someone with words, ask Allah's forgiveness — and where possible, theirs.
gheebat.hadithText: Do you know what backbiting is? It is mentioning your brother in a manner he dislikes.

callFamily.p1: {calls} times a year you reach out — a thread that holds.
callFamily.p2: {decadeCalls} reaching-outs across a decade. People feel them.
callFamily.c1: Parents over 60 have a finite number of weekends left — visits and calls become the most precious currency you have.
callFamily.c2: Children grow up in one direction. The version of them you missed talking to today doesn't return.
callFamily.r1: Call a parent or sibling now. Even if it's awkward. Especially if it's awkward.
callFamily.r2: Schedule a recurring time — Friday after Asr, Sunday morning. Make it a fixed feature, not a feeling.
callFamily.hadithText: Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him be generous to his guest. Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him uphold the ties of kinship.

diamondExtra.islamic: One year of consistency. May this be the beginning, not the peak.
diamondExtra.universal: One year of consistency. The first year is the hardest. Carry on.

spark.islamic: Every journey to Allah begins with a single step.
spark.universal: Every great fire began as a single spark.

flame.islamic: Seven days. The Prophet ﷺ said the most beloved deeds to Allah are the consistent ones — and you just proved it.
flame.universal: Seven days. You showed up when most people quit by day three. This is who you really are.

burn.islamic: Twenty-one days. Allah opens the door for those who knock with consistency. The habit is now wired into you.
burn.universal: Twenty-one days. Your brain has wired the habit. It's no longer something you do — it's becoming who you are.

blaze.islamic: Two months. You no longer choose this each day — you ARE someone who does this. Alhamdulillah.
blaze.universal: Two months. You stopped trying and started being. The identity is real now.

inferno.islamic: Four months. Allah loves the consistent, even if small. You are now of those He loves.
inferno.universal: Four months. While others started and quit a dozen times, you became unstoppable. This is rare.

sacred.islamic: By Allah's mercy, you are no longer building a habit — you are living an identity. The year-mark is near.
sacred.universal: Most people never reach this. You are no longer building a habit — you ARE this habit. The year-mark is near.

diamond.islamic: One full year. The fire of effort has cooled into something eternal. By Allah's mercy, you have become this habit — and it has become you.
diamond.universal: One full year. The fire is gone. What remains is permanent. You did not just build a habit — you forged who you are.

futureSelf.emptyState: Start any habit today. By tomorrow, your first spark will light here.
futureSelf.thirtyDayHint: How consistent you've been per category.
futureSelf.smallestBody: What's the smallest thing you can do today that would have the biggest impact on your life?
futureSelf.noWhy: Add the reason that drives you.
futureSelf.noFutureSelfName: Give your future self a name.

quotes.q0: 10 pages a day → 12 books a year → 120 books in a decade. Still a small daily act, but a transformed life.
quotes.q1: 30 minutes of movement daily → 182 hours a year — enough to walk from London to Paris twice.
quotes.q2: 1 hour less scrolling daily → 15 full days reclaimed every year.
quotes.q3: 5 pages of Quran daily → completes the Quran every 4 months. Three khatmas a year.
quotes.q4: Tahajjud once a week → 52 nights of closeness with your Lord this year.
quotes.q5: 1 act of sadaqah daily → 365 acts a year. Charity that keeps giving.
quotes.q6: 15 minutes of learning daily → 91 hours of mastery a year. Compounded over a decade, a new identity.
quotes.q7: “The most beloved deeds to Allah are those that are consistent, even if small.” — Sahih al-Bukhari 6464
quotes.q8: You are a gainer when today is better than yesterday. — Prophetic wisdom
quotes.q9: “The strong one is not the wrestler — he is the one who controls himself when angry.” — Bukhari & Muslim
quotes.q10: “Truthfulness leads to virtue. Stay truthful until you are written as a truthful one.” — Bukhari & Muslim
quotes.q11: “Sitting alone is better than sitting with the bad — and sitting with the good is better than sitting alone.” — Hadith

presets.fivePrayers: Five daily prayers in their time
